question: What does the timer.unref() method do?

options.text: Allows the Node.js process to exit even if the timer is still active
Removes the timer from memory
options.explanation: unref() doesn't remove the timer — it will still fire if the process is alive. unref() just won't keep the process running

options.text: Pauses the timer until ref() is called
options.explanation: unref() doesn't pause the timer. It only affects whether the timer keeps the process from exiting

options.text: Lowers the timer's priority in the Event Loop
options.explanation: unref() doesn't affect execution priority. It only manages the process lifecycle

question: Why is AbortSignal.timeout(5000) better than manual setTimeout + abort?

options.text: Creates a signal that auto-aborts after 5 seconds without manual AbortController and timer management
It's faster because it uses native code
options.explanation: Performance is roughly the same. The advantage is convenience and no manual resource management

options.text: It allows cancelling the timeout early
options.explanation: AbortSignal.timeout() cannot be cancelled — you need AbortController for that. Its advantage is simplicity for one-shot timeouts

options.text: It automatically retries the operation on timeout
options.explanation: AbortSignal.timeout() doesn't implement retry. It simply cancels the operation when time expires

question: Which of the following implement Symbol.asyncIterator in Node.js?

options.text: Readable streams (fs.createReadStream)
readline.Interface
Result of events.on(emitter, event)
Regular arrays

question: Why can setInterval with an async callback cause problems?

options.text: If the operation takes longer than the interval, calls will overlap
Async callbacks are not supported in setInterval
options.explanation: setInterval accepts async callbacks but doesn't await them before the next invocation

options.text: setInterval automatically cancels on promise rejection
options.explanation: setInterval doesn't track promises — a rejected Promise becomes an unhandled rejection while the interval continues

options.text: V8 cannot optimize async callbacks in timers
options.explanation: V8 optimizes async functions the same as regular ones. The issue is setInterval semantics, not performance

options.text: Resets the timer, restarting the countdown without recreating the object
Updates the timer's callback function
options.explanation: refresh() doesn't change the callback — it only resets the time until firing

options.text: Restarts the timer with a new interval
options.explanation: refresh() preserves the original interval. A new timer is needed to change the interval

options.text: Force-invokes the timer's callback
options.explanation: refresh() doesn't invoke the callback — it only restarts the countdown
